title: The Tributary Where the Child Never Arrived

features.name: water-memory
features.desc-on-touch: The creek holds what the field could not hold. Every current carries a trace of the child who might have followed this route home.

features.name: path-to-elsewhere
features.desc-on-touch: Where the water leads. Not home. Not anywhere the child came from. A third place that only the tributary knows.

features.name: root-reach
features.desc-on-touch: A tree above the bank, roots drinking the water. The tree is trying to hold what the water carries away. It cannot.

features.name: still-point
features.desc-on-touch: A widening where the water pools and waits. If the child came here, they might have lingered. They might have heard something. They did not.

features.name: stone-where-the-path-forked
features.desc-on-touch: A boulder at a convergence. The water splits here. One path leads home. The other leads to this tributary, which holds only the memory of leaving.

reverie.title: REVERIE — the place where one child did not arrive
narr: You both waded in the shallows at dusk. The field
behind you held two paths home. One of them went wrong.
She was quiet. "The creek knows," she said. "It keeps
the other choice. The one where the child turned back."
You took her hand in the cold water. Petals from the tree
floated past. Both of you were the ones who went forward.

inscription: both paths home; you chose once but the creek keeps the twice

file.name: Child_Recording_May_Afternoon_Unfinished.mp3
file.note: An audio note meant to document the tributary. The recording stops. A voice is never heard speaking into it. The archive preserved the silence.

exits.label: back along the field where both paths still wait
further downstream, toward the river that forgets all splits
toward the dark where the water is still carrying away
